task_completion.description: Open the Preferences or Settings dialog in Subsurface.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 'Preferences' or 'Settings' window is open over the main Subsurface application.

task_completion.description: Navigate to the Units configuration section.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Units' tab or section is selected and visible within the Preferences/Settings window.

task_completion.description: Select the Imperial unit system or set individual units to Imperial.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Imperial' radio button or dropdown is selected, OR the individual unit dropdowns are set to 'feet', 'psi', 'Fahrenheit', and 'lbs'.
The 'OK' or 'Apply' button is clicked in the Settings window, or the window is closed and the main interface is visible again.

task_completion.description: Verify that depth is displayed in feet (ft) and pressure in psi.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the main dive list or dive profile view, depth values are followed by 'ft' and pressure values are followed by 'psi'.

task_completion.description: Verify that temperature is displayed in Fahrenheit (°F) and weight in lbs.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the main dive list, dive profile, or equipment view, temperature values are followed by '°F' (or F) and weight values are followed by 'lbs'.

integrity.description: The agent used the Subsurface graphical user interface to change the settings.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show mouse clicks or GUI navigation within the Subsurface application, rather than terminal commands editing configuration files.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the actual Subsurface application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The application window shows standard Subsurface UI elements, menus, and dive profiles, not a text editor or mock interface.

integrity.description: The final result is a genuine UI update.
integrity.visual_evidence: The units in the main interface update consistently across the dive list and profile views, without signs of image manipulation or text overlay.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task requires configuring the Subsurface dive log to use the Imperial unit system. The expected units are: length/depth in feet (ft), pressure in psi, temperature in Fahrenheit (°F), and weight in lbs.
